channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: A chatbot entirely powered by humans, not artificial intelligence? This Chilean community shows why
item.description: About 50 members of a community outside Chile’s capital spent Saturday trying their best to power an entirely human-operated chatbot that could answer questions and make silly pictures on command, in a message to highlight the environmental toll and water usage of artificial intelligence data center
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: This smart service saves memorable podcast moments in your inbox
item.description: Listen, I don’t know about you, but I’m generally not so big on listening. I tend to be more of a “words in front of my eyes” kind of guy when it comes to taking in information (which, as I’ve come to learn, also means I’m “an old person” by modern-day standards—hey, I’m okay with that).
item.dc:creator: JR Raphael, Fast Company

item.title: Apple's iPhone sales surge to new quarterly high despite early missteps in artificial intelligence
item.description: Apple’s iPhone sales soared to a new quarterly record during the holiday season, despite artificial intelligence blunders that prompted the technology trendsetter to get a helping hand from Google.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The Future of Critical Infrastructure: Strategies for IT/OT Security, Workforce Readiness, and Trust
item.description: During this webinar, government and industry experts will discuss how agencies are addressing IT/OT security and resilience through workforce development, pilot programs and emerging technologies.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Nvidia: Latest news and insights
item.description: More processor coverage on Network World: Intel news and insights | AMD news and insights With its legacy of innovation in GPU technology, Nvidia has become a dominant force in the AI market. Nvidia’s partners read like a technology who’s who list – e.g.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Crooks are hijacking and reselling AI infrastructure: Report
item.description: For years, CSOs have worried about their IT infrastructure being used for unauthorized cryptomining. Now, say researchers, they’d better start worrying about crooks hijacking and reselling access to exposed corporate AI infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: WhatsApp gets new security features
item.description: The popular messaging app WhatsApp got a range of new security features Tuesday with the launch of “Strict Account Settings.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How two companies are moving AI prototypes to production
item.description: With many AI projects failing, there’s no one-size-fits-all formula for advancing AI proofs of concept to real-world use in the corporate world. But two companies, Ernst & Young (EY) and Lumen, have had success — though they’ve tackled the issue in dramatically different ways.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Grubhub confirms data breach amid extortion claims
item.description: Grubhub data breach confirmed as sources say ShinyHunters group allegedly demands Bitcoin payment to prevent release of stolen customer support data.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AI robot brings emotional care to pets
item.description: Aura AI pet robot tracks behavior changes to detect when pets feel excited, anxious or relaxed. Tuya Smart's device offers laser play, treat dispensing and voice interaction.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Intel wrestling with CPU supply shortage
item.description: Intel said that it is facing a CPU shortage, particularly affecting the data center/server business, but that it expects the problem to peak this quarter and ease throughout the year.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Google Fast Pair flaw lets hackers hijack headphones
item.description: Google responds to WhisperPair Fast Pair security flaws by releasing patches to manufacturers and updating certification requirements for protection.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
